title: iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni Technical Report
author: Yuan Zhang, Jingfei Ni, Guanchen Lu, Shiqi Zhang, Qingshan Xu, Chi Liu, Xin Nie, Wenjie Xu, Lin Gao, Zhiyuan Cheng, Mingxin Zhou, Jiajia Wu, Diyuan Liu, Jia Pan, Chao Ji
raw_description: arXiv:2607.02542v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: General-purpose embodied agents must understand multimodal instructions, anticipate how their environment will evolve, and produce precise control actions over extended horizons. Existing approaches typically specialize in visual-language reasoning, video-based world modeling, or action generation, while cascaded pipelines that first synthesize future observations and then infer actions can introduce interface bottlenecks and compound prediction errors. We present iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni, a unified multimodal foundation model that jointly models vision(videos and images), language, and action within a single Omni framework. Its modality-specific visual-language, video-generation, and action-generation components communicate through shared multimodal self-attention. This design establishes brain-cerebellum collaboration: the vision-language modeland video generation model form a high-level brain for instruction understanding, task planning,
summary: 提出iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni统一多模态基础模型，联合视觉、语言和动作，通过共享自注意力实现脑-小脑协作，用于通用具身智能体的指令理解、世界建模和长程控制。
analysis_note: 技术突破显著，统一框架解决级联误差，脑-小脑协作设计新颖，具身智能领域重要进展。
